title: papaver rhoeas f. integrifolium
intro: Papaver rhoeas f. integrifolium, also known as the red poppy or Shirley poppy, is a flowering plant species that is a variant of the common poppy (Papaver rhoeas). It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as thebaine and morphinan. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing pain, improving sleep, and providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Papaver rhoeas f. integrifolium can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to note that its safety and efficacy should be evaluated under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
